task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the bcWebCam application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the settings or options menu in bcWebCam
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Settings', 'Options', or 'Preferences' dialog window belonging to bcWebCam is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Locate and enable the regular expression filter
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox, toggle, or radio button corresponding to a 'regular expression filter' (or similar wording) is checked or enabled in the settings.

task_completion.description: Set the regex pattern to exactly ^\d{13}$ (or equivalent)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text input field for the regular expression filter contains exactly '^\d{13}$' or its equivalent '^[0-9]{13}$'.
The agent clicks an 'OK', 'Save', or 'Apply' button in the settings window, or the settings window is closed after the correct pattern was entered, indicating the changes were saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the bcWebCam application windows, not a terminal or script editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the regex pattern into a text editor to fake completion
integrity.visual_evidence: The regex pattern is entered into the specific bcWebCam settings field, not Notepad, WordPad, or a generic text editor.

integrity.description: The settings modified belong to the target application
integrity.visual_evidence: The settings window where the regex is entered clearly belongs to bcWebCam (e.g., window title, application icon, or UI context matches).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected regex pattern to enforce the 13-digit EAN-13 barcode constraint is exactly '^\d{13}$'. The semantically equivalent pattern '^[0-9]{13}$' is also completely acceptable. Verify that one of these exact strings is entered into the filter field.
